title: Box Office: Leonardo DiCaprio's One Battle After Another Opens with $22 Million
text: Director Paul Thomas Anderson's comedic action thriller topped the box office with a $22.4 million domestic opening from 3,634 theaters, adding $26.1 million internationally for a $48.5 million global total. The film stars DiCaprio as an ex-revolutionary in hiding with his daughter until an old enemy resurfaces. Industry analysts offered mixed reactions to the debut. One consultant noted: "This movie has a chance of getting to profitability if it lasts long enough in theaters and/or overperforms abroad." The $130 million production with a $70 million marketing budget faces high stakes, requiring approximately $300 million globally to break even due to standard 50-50 theater revenue splits. Audiences gave the film an "A" CinemaScore grade, while critics awarded it 96% on Rotten Tomatoes. Premium formats (Imax, Dolby, VistaVision) generated 51% of domestic earnings, suggesting strong potential for sustained theatrical performance. Initial ticket buyers skewed male (65%) and older than 25 (42%). DiCaprio's last theatrical outing, Martin Scorsese's "Killers of the Flower Moon," opened to $23 million and earned $158 million globally. Universal's "Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie" earned second place with $13.5 million domestically, while Lionsgate's "The Strangers: Chapter 2" underperformed with $5.9 million.
